figures.caption: Figure 4: React F1 by prompt granularity and model.
Table 3: React baseline results. All values are file-level scores.

Figure 6: React baseline, task-level extreme outcomes. Each cell reports the fraction of historical PR tasks that land in an exact success or exact failure state. The largest movement is from Minimal/Concise to Contextual/Guided: better prompts sharply reduce both complete misses (Recall= 0) and spurious localization failures (Precision= 0), while increasing exact localization (Recall= 1, F1= 1).

Figure 7: React baseline, task-level F1 distributions. The distribution does not simply shift upward smoothly; rather, prompt strengthening moves substantial probability mass out of the zero-performance bin and into the high-F1 and exact-match bins.

Figure 1: Time-consistent benchmark pipeline. A repository is snapshotted at 𝑇0 to construct repository knowledge using only pre-𝑇0 information. Pull requests merged during (𝑇0, 𝑇1] are converted into natural-language tasks. The same SWE agent is then evaluated in matched conditions with and without repository-derived code knowledge, and outputs are compared against the historical ground-truth pull requests.

Figure 8: DragonFly baseline, task-level extreme outcomes. Each cell reports the fraction of historical PR tasks that land in an exact success or exact failure state. As in React, stronger prompts sharply reduce both complete misses (Recall= 0) and spurious localization failures (Precision= 0), while increasing exact localization (Recall= 1, F1= 1). Gray cells indicate combinations for which task-level detail exports are unavailable in the current archive.
Figure 9: DragonFly baseline, task-level F1 distributions. As on React, prompt strengthening moves substantial probability mass out of the zero-performance bin and into high-F1 and exact-match bins.
Figure 10: Cross-repository comparison. Stronger prompts improve localization across both repositories, and the absolute gain from Minimal to Guided prompting is large for every model.
Table 1: Benchmark dataset and evaluation configuration. The protocol itself supports matched knowledge-augmentedvs-baseline comparison; the present section reports only the baseline arm.
Table 2: DragonFly baseline results. All values are file-level scores.
Figure 2: DragonFly F1 by prompt granularity and model.
